summary: Agent1 summarizes that thumb sucking continues being a common behavior up to approximately four years due to its prevalence among children before gradually tapering off as they grow older; thus, it can be deemed typical for much longer than just early infancy but does start reducing notably after two or three years when most kids stop doing so voluntarily without intervention from caregivers if not persistent beyond these ages. The primary focus here should remain consistent across both iterations while ensuring clarity regarding duration and frequency within pediatric development stages related directly back towards answering whether such habits are still seen clinically speaking under "normal" circumstances well into toddlerhood rather than strictly limited only post-infancy periods alone where other factors might come more prominently into play influencing decisions made concerning interventions needed at later developmental milestones instead purely based upon initial onset timing noted earlier herein
